title: multi-column RANGE COLUMNS partitioning  prune looks strange
description: ## Bug Report The partition prune in multi-column RANGE COLUMNS looks strange. Don't known if it is a bug ### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required) DDL ``` CREATE TABLE tk ( valid_until datetime, name varchar(255) CHARACTER SET ascii, notes text ) PARTITION BY RANGE COLUMNS(name,valid_until) (PARTITION `p2022-g` VALUES LESS THAN ('G','2023-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2023-g` VALUES LESS THAN ('G','2024-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2024-g` VALUES LESS THAN ('G','2025-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2022-m` VALUES LESS THAN ('M','2023-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2023-m` VALUES LESS THAN ('M','2024-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2024-m` VALUES LESS THAN ('M','2025-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2022-s` VALUES LESS THAN ('S','2023-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2023-s` VALUES LESS THAN ('S','2024-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2024-s` VALUES LESS THAN ('S','2025-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2022-` VALUES LESS THAN (0x7f,'2023-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2023-` VALUES LESS THAN (0x7f,'2024-01-01 00:00:00'), PARTITION `p2024-` VALUES LESS THAN (0x7f,'2025-01-01 00:00:00')) ``` Query ``` select * from tk where name = 'G' ``` why is p2023-g selected? I think this query's range is ('G','')， why can the p2023-g contain this range? ### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required) v6.5.0
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tk2 (valid_until DATETIME, name TEXT NOT NULL, notes TEXT);
INSERT INTO tk2 (valid_until, name, notes) VALUES ('2023-01-01 12:00:00', 'G', 'Note 1');
INSERT INTO tk2 (valid_until, name, notes) VALUES ('2023-01-01 12:00:00', 'M', 'Note 2');
SELECT * FROM tk2 WHERE name = 'G' AND valid_until < '2024-01-01 00:00:00';
SELECT * FROM tk2 WHERE name IS NULL OR valid_until IS NULL;
